judge_reason: The descriptions give different specific verbatim text for the same stamps (e.g., 'RECEIVED INTERNAL SECURITY NS FBI' vs 'RECEIVED ... INTERNAL SECURITY SN FBI', and 'RECEIVED ESPIONAGE FBI' vs 'SEC'D ESPIONAGE FBI').
original_image_tags: Aged, off-white paper page with two dark punch holes at the top. The top section contains a typed name and address. Below it is a handwritten postscript in cursive. The bottom third of the page is covered with multiple blue ink stamped marks. One vertical stamp on the right reads: "OCT 24 5 06 PM '52 RECE'D ESPIONAGE FBI U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE". A diagonal stamp below it reads: "RECEIVED INTERNAL SECURITY NS FBI OCT 24 2 28 PM '52". Another diagonal stamp to the left reads: "NOV 3 5 03 PM '52 RECE'D ESPIONAGE FBI U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE". The word "RECEIVED" is partially visible at the very bottom edge.
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: Scanned handwritten letter page with black binder holes at the top and a purple/blue library stamp near the bottom reading “RECEIVED,” “NETMANS-SECURITY,” “FBI,” “DOC 24 28 PM ’52,” and “NOT FOR PUBLIC RECORD.”*
